title: Proofpoint TAP VIP and Executive Mailbox Targeted Threat Analysis
dataSources: `sourcetype=proofpoint_tap_siem` TAP SIEM JSON correlated to `vip_mailboxes.csv` (canonical executive addresses)
app: Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk (Splunkbase 3681)
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint_tap_siem" earliest=-30d
| eval rcpt=lower(coalesce(recipient, Recipient, rcpt_to, ""))
| lookup vip_mailboxes.csv email AS rcpt OUTPUT title org_unit
| where isnotnull(title)
| eval cls=coalesce(classification, Classification, "")
| eval threat=coalesce(threatType, ThreatType, "")
| stats count values(cls) as classifications values(threat) as threat_types values(subject) as subjects by rcpt title
| sort -count
description: Executives face disproportionate spear-phishing and BEC risk. Filtering TAP telemetry to a curated VIP roster surfaces targeted campaigns, attachment malware, and malicious URLs aimed at leadership mailboxes.
value: Enables executive-protection programs with measurable threat volume per leader and informs close-hold briefings without drowning analysts in org-wide noise.
implementation: (1) Maintain `vip_mailboxes.csv` under change control with HR or EA teams. (2) Normalize recipient fields for multi-recipient messages (`mvzip` / `mvexpand` if needed). (3) Restrict dashboard ACLs. (4) Map to physical protection for travel risk. (5) Review monthly with CISO staff.
visualization: Table (recipient, title, threat counts), heatmap (exec × threat type), single-value (VIP threats this week).
grandmaExplanation: We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.
knownFalsePositives: High-touch executives receive more total mail; not every targeted thread is a new campaign.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
